Name: Certificate-Based Authentication Persistence
Description:
This indicator looks for the presence of specific Entra ID Microsoft Graph app roles and permissions that, when combined, can enable a user to establish persistence through certificate-based authentication (CBA).

Required permissions

  • RoleManagement.Read.Directory
  • RoleManagement.Read.All
  • Directory.Read.All
  • Application.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise: If an attacker is able to gain access to an Account with Authentication Policy Administrator role or an application with a combination of Policy.ReadWrite.AuthenticationMethod and Organization.ReadWrite.All permissions, they can log into Entra ID as any user, even a Global Administrator.
ResultMessage: Found {0} principals with at least one of the required permissions to be able to persist using CBA.
Remediation:
To mitigate the risk associated with Certificate-based authentication (CBA) persistence, it is recommended that the organization review and evaluate the necessity of the permissions and role assignments that can be leveraged for a CBA attack.

Limit the assignment of the following permissions only to trusted apps that need the ability to add a Root CA if CBA is enabled:

  • Authentication Policy Administrator
  • Application with Policy.ReadWrite.AuthenticationMethod and Organization.ReadWrite.All permissions

Remove the following roles and permissions from any account that does not require them for operational purposes:

  • Privileged Role Administrator
  • Privileged Authentication Administrator
  • RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory
  • AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All
